messages.content: On Nov 13, 2023, while checking on the bank reconciliations of September, I came upon unmatched receipts from sales invoices and from there found accounts that have notes that they were checked by accounting for the payments and found some that even have receipts in the software, indicating that these were checked and verified through bank reconciliation. Upon further checking of the unmatched receipts, I found the discrepancies very odd as the payments indicated weekday payments. Upon further checking, I came through accounts under Wilhelmina Manalaysay where I found around 3 payments for year 2023 (June 4 at BDO, August 25 at Metrobank, Sept 9 at BDO). I randomly checked through the payments and banks for these payments and to my surprise and dismay, I could not find any of the payments in the banks. All of these were under the accounts of Tricia of Sales. But all of these seem to be verified through accounting because the August and September I checked had receipts in the software.  Upon further checking through the client by informing that I am doing a random check in auditing, I found out that the payments for these were transferred, not to the company account, but to the personal BDO account of our salesperson.  I am questioning how come these were not seen and audited properly by the accounting people involved, and even had receipts confirming that they were paid and checked in the bank. please revise

On November 13, 2023, while reviewing the bank reconciliations for September, I discovered several unmatched receipts from sales invoices. This prompted me to investigate further, leading me to accounts that had notes indicating they were reviewed by the accounting department for payment verification. Surprisingly, some of these accounts even had receipts documented in the software, suggesting that they were thoroughly checked and confirmed during the bank reconciliation process.

However, as I examined the unmatched receipts more closely, I noticed some peculiar discrepancies. Specifically, the payments seemed to have been made on weekdays, which raised concerns. Upon conducting a deeper investigation, I stumbled upon several accounts under the name of Wilhelmina Manalaysay. These accounts revealed three payments made in the year 2023, namely on June 4 at BDO, August 25 at Metrobank, and September 9 at BDO. Intrigued, I decided to randomly verify these payments by contacting the respective banks. To my surprise and dismay, none of these payments could be traced in any of the banks.

Strangely, all these payments were linked to the accounts of Tricia, a member of the Sales team. What perplexes me further is that these transactions appeared to have been authenticated by the accounting department, as evidenced by the presence of receipts in the software for both August and September. 

To gain a better understanding of the situation, I reached out to the client, informing them that I was conducting a random audit check. It was during this communication that I discovered the payments had been transferred to our salesperson's personal BDO account, rather than the company's official account. This revelation left me questioning how these transactions went unnoticed and were not adequately audited by the accounting personnel involved. Additionally, it is bewildering that receipts were issued to confirm these payments were checked and verified during the bank reconciliation process, despite the irregularities surrounding them.

November 21, 2023

Upon checking of the supposedly bank reconciliations submitted by Emily from year 2021 up to March 2023, there was no discrepancies escalated to management and owners when in fact, the company was being committed fraud and money was being laundered from within the company.  Supposedly, her work was meant to find discrepancies in the payments against the sales invoices from the orders taken by the salespersons.

November 21, 2023

During my examination of the bank reconciliations provided by Emily, covering the period from 2021 up to March 2023, I made a significant and alarming discovery. It became evident that there were no discrepancies reported or escalated to management and the owners, despite the fact that the company was indeed experiencing fraudulent activities and internal money laundering. Emily's assigned responsibility was to identify any anomalies or inconsistencies between the payments received and the corresponding sales invoices generated from the orders placed by the sales team.

This oversight raises serious concerns about Emily's performance and adherence to her duties. It appears that she either failed to identify and report the fraudulent transactions or deliberately neglected to inform higher authorities about the suspicious activities taking place within the company. Either way, her actions (or lack thereof) have had severe consequences for the organization.

The absence of proper detection and reporting mechanisms for fraudulent activities within the company, particularly in financial transactions and money laundering, highlights a critical weakness in the internal control system. Investigating the extent of the fraud and the individuals involved will be of utmost importance to mitigate the financial loss incurred and prevent any future occurrences.

I recommend an immediate and thorough investigation into the matter, including a review of Emily's performance, to determine the causes behind this systemic failure. It is imperative that appropriate measures are implemented to enhance internal controls, ensuring the early detection and prevention of fraudulent activities within the company.

Should you require any further information or assistance in conducting the investigation or implementing control measures, please do not hesitate to reach out.

Sincerely,

[Your Name - AI Assistant]
